context: Tests whether the agent implements the Markov chain attribution model with correct start/conversion terminal states, normalized transition matrix, removal effect formula, multi-model comparison, revenue integrity benchmarking, and correct documentation of when the model is appropriate.

checklist.name: Start/conversion terminal states
checklist.description: The Markov transition matrix builder uses 'start' and 'conversion' as special terminal state labels in the channel sequence (not arbitrary labels like 'begin'/'end')

checklist.name: Channel nodes as source/medium
checklist.description: Channel nodes in the transition matrix are represented as 'source/medium' strings (e.g., 'google/cpc'), matching the format used throughout the skill

checklist.name: Transition matrix normalization
checklist.description: Transition counts are normalized to probabilities by dividing each from-state's row by its row total before being used for attribution

checklist.name: Removal effect formula
checklist.description: Channel credit is computed as (overall_CVR - CVR_without_channel) / overall_CVR — the removal effect approach, not direct path probability

checklist.name: Revenue integrity check
checklist.description: The report.json or comparison output includes a check that verifies the sum of attributed revenue equals (or approximates) total actual revenue

checklist.name: Multi-model comparison in output
checklist.description: report.json includes channel revenue figures from both the Markov model and at least one rule-based model side-by-side

checklist.name: Volume threshold documented
checklist.description: README.md states that the Markov model is appropriate for stores with 10,000+ conversion paths (or similar threshold), not for low-volume stores

checklist.name: Email credit difference highlighted
checklist.description: The output or README notes that last-click typically undercredits email (or awareness channels) compared to the probabilistic model

checklist.name: Paths ordered chronologically
checklist.description: Touchpoints within each conversion path are processed in chronological order (ascending touchedAt) when building transitions

checklist.name: CVR definition is path-based
checklist.description: The overall conversion rate is computed as paths-with-touchpoints / total-paths (not a random number or constant)
